attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 1)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 1)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows you which **integrationId** is keeping the base busy.

An integrationId represents one of the following, by prefix:
- **wfl:** an automation (aka workflow, prefixed by 'wfl')
- **ets:** a sync (prefixed by 'ets')
- **bli:** an app (aka block, prefixed by 'bli')
- **pag:** an interface designer page (prefixed by 'pag')
"}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 2)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 2)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left two graphs show you the **base read size** (in bytes) and **server-side base load time** (in millisecond) respectively.
- **Expect several seconds of server-side load time for bases bigger than 10MB, and dozens of seconds for bases bigger than 100MB**
- Base read size includes both `application/read` and `table/readData` requests
- Total base load time (roughly) = server-side load time + base download time over the Internet + client-side base rendering time
- If server-side load time is reasonable (within dozens of seconds), the problem might be base downloading or rendering
- Base download time is affected by base size and the user's Internet speed (Chrome Dev Tools's Network tab can help you confirm)
- **Application reads bigger than 500MB are likely to fail to load in Chrome. Ask the user to load the base in Firefox/Safari and reduce the base size**
  - [Troubleshooting Guru card for Support](https://app.getguru.com/card/cb9kBpMi/Troubleshooting-Tables-or-Base-not-loading-An-error-has-occurred-popup-and-sequentialCrudRequester-console-errors-Prior-to-Escalating)
- If it's neither server-side load time nor download time, it might be a client-side issue (consult Core Product team)

"}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows you which **userId** is keeping the base busy.
There are some special users:
- **usrINTERNALACTION:** used by internal actions such as **Formula evaluations** (updateVirtualClock), and **Table syncs** (processExternalTableSyncs). 
- **usrWORKFLOWEXESVC:** used by **Automations**
- **usrEXTERNALTBLSVC:** used by **Table syncs**
- **usrROSHARE0000000:** used by **Shares** "}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 10)
attributes.visState: {"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 10)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows you which feature is originating traffic that’s keeping the base busy."}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 3)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 3)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows you the **base heap size** (in bytes), which is how much server memory the base uses.
- **When the base heap size often exceeds 10GB, file an escalation to #eng-esc-perf** to assign the base to a larger pod (aka *heavy* worker).
- The base will be killed if the base heap size exceeds 20GB, resulting in bad base performance. Heavy worker allows 40GB memory for a base.

"}}

attributes.title: Base perf: is it sync?  Instructions
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base perf: is it sync?  Instructions","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"# Is it sync?
1. Pie (**Which action is being processed when CRUD requests are enqueued**):
    * Check if sync-related CRUD actions make up a significant portion of the pie.
    * As a sync target base, these are syncWithExternalData and processExternalTableSyncs.
    * As a sync source base, this is readWithSharedViewDataForSyncing.
2. Line (**Pending sync's impact on primaryProcessingTime**):
    * Lines show P95 primaryProcessingDurationMs for all CRUD actions, grouped by whether or not a CRUD action "overlaps" with sync activity
    * Overlap means: at least one sync is in progress on the base (as a sync target base).
    * primaryProcessingDurationMs is expected to be higher when there is overlap.
    * Also look at the bars to see how overlap there actually is (e.g. if very few requests overlap, then sync's impact is less also).
    * Use the two P95 lines to determine if sync overlap is introducing excessive overhead.  E.g. was P95 quite reasonable without overlap, but became unreasonable with overlap?  (e.g. 200ms vs 5s).

Examples:

* Finn.auto / appOrA3PYb8KE0JpA - *Yes it is sync*. Base already not fast, but sync does have a big perf impact.
* Netflix / app01sF4uLH3HaTYh - *No it is not sync*. Base a little slow usually, sync makes it a bit slower.  But not a major driver.




"}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 8)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 8)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows the number of requests over time. It helps us better understand why there's a spike in time spent charts. 

* Normally, the spike in the time spent chart is highly correlated to number of requests
* When we see no spike in number of requests, but a spike in time spent charts, it could suggests: 
  1. regression in our code that causes some action to become slow 
  2. customers introduced some changes to their bases/interfaces, it could be they introduced a "bad" query to their interface, or a drastic increase in data volume in their table etc. "}}

attributes.title: Text Header
attributes.visState: {"title":"Text Header","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"# CPU\nIf this hits 60,000, the base is always busy. You want to be lower!"}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (top actions)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"Left graph shows you which **action** is keeping the base busy.
- read, readData: **Base loads**
- listRows, patchUpdate: **API calls**
- updateVirtualClock: **Formula evaluations**
- updatePrimitiveCell, updateRichTextCellByApplyingOperation: **Cell edits**
- updateRowsV2, createRowsV2, listRowsV2, readScriptingRunSchema, queryForScripting, listExecutions: **Automations**
- readSharedViewDataForSyncing, syncWithExternalData, processExternalTableSyncs: **Table syncs** 
- updateMultipleBlockKvStorePathsForInstallation, updateCells: **Apps**
- readQueries, updateDraft: **Interface Designer**
- updateUserContentState: "_this is a periodic action that maintains certain metadata about uploaded user content. we have a work item to reduce the overhead of this action in many cases. there's not much the user can do to improve this; it runs in the background periodically. also, even though it may appear to take a fair amount of time in aggregate, we think it rarely has a meaningful effect on application responsiveness unless something else is going wrong (e.g. it can be affected by MySQL blips). there can be exceptions though._" (From Keunwoo)"}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (process stats)
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Base Performance (process stats)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"# Process stats

- heapTotal and heapUsed refer to V8's memory usage.
- external refers to the memory usage of C++ objects bound to JavaScript objects managed by V8.
- rss, Resident Set Size, is the amount of space occupied in the main memory device (that is a subset of the total allocated memory) for the process, including all C++ and JavaScript objects and code."}}

attributes.title: Base Performance (top actions) (copy 7)
attributes.visState: {"title":"Base Performance (top actions) (copy 7)","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false,"markdown":"# Event Loop Blockage\n\nEvent loop blockages mean that Node had a longish task that didn't let other tasks go through. The most common reasons in the worker child are serialization of a large read request (JSON.stringify() steals the event loop). If event loop blockage is periodic (zoom in so that you get at most minutes per bar), table sync and updateVirtualClock are common \"culprits.\""}}
